para_trans: 0 to 100 under Mukhyamantri Jal Sanrakshan Yojana
Maintenance and Repair of H.S.K. Projects
Distribution of funds for works: G. Kolhapur (Rest of Maharashtra Division)
Government of Maharashtra
Soil and Water Conservation Department
Government Decision No: Amendment-2026 / No.94 (E-1567075) / Ministry of Water-4, Mumbai-400032.
Date: 23rd March, 2026.
Reference: - 1) Department of Finance, Government Circular No. Finance-2025 / Q. No.44 / Finance-3, dated 7th April, 2025 and 29th January, 2026 and dated 12th March, 2026.
2) Regional Water Conservation Officer, Soil and Water Conservation Department, Pune
Office letter no. Lesha / 210, dated January 20, 2026.
Proposal: - Preservation of minor irrigation (water harvesting) projects with irrigation potential of 0 to 100 hectares and

Demand for Repair Plan No. ZH-5, Account Head 4702 A046 was budgeted at Rs 125.00 crore in the year 2025-26. Now the reference of the Finance Department is No. 1 Out of the funds made available under the revised estimate limit as per the circular herein, Reference No. 2 The following decision has been taken on the fund demand proposal here.
The Government's Decisions:
Shahapur Repair of Pazar Lake in Panhala Taluka of Kolhapur District.
Rs. 73.23 Lakh (Rs.) to pay for maintenance and repair work of the project
seventy-three lakh twenty-three thousand only) to distribute the funds in a phased manner,
Government approval is being granted.
The aforesaid funds are being disbursed subject to the following terms and conditions: -

Funds should be disbursed on a payment basis, ensuring quality of repair work, b) not exceeding the sanctioned price, c) if funds remain unutilized for any reason, the amount should be immediately returned to the government without any reciprocal distribution, e) strictly follow the guidelines in the relevant circular of the Department of Finance regarding fund disbursement.
e) Ensure that the procedures and instructions laid down under the Government decision on the Mukhyamantri Jal Sanrakshan Yojana are complied with.
3. The amount disbursed by it to the Regional Water Conservation Officer, Mrid and
The Water Conservation Department, Pune has taken over the respective district water conservation under its jurisdiction.
It should be delivered to the authorities quickly.
4. The expenditure on this shall be sub-scribed to the headings below and shall be deemed to be
2025-26 to be subtracted from the approved provision for this financial year.
Demand No. ZH-5,
4702, Capital expenditure on minor irrigation
101, surface water
(2) Maintenance and repair.
(02) (01) Minor Irrigation Schemes
Conservation and Repair of Irrigation Potential from 0 to 100 hectares (Programme) (4702A046)
53, Major constructions
5. Submitting utilization certificates of the above expenses to the Accountant General's office.
Copies of the same should be made available to the government along with a certificate of completion of the repair.
6. Responsibility for coordination of funds disbursed by the respective regions.
The Office of the Water Conservation Officer shall be the Office of the Additional Commissioner and the Chief Engineer, Soil and
Water Conservation, Regional Area, Pune will be the coordinating authority for these matters.
7. Presentation Order Reference No. 1 As per the instructions in the circular here.
Also, the Informal Reference No. 181 / Expenditure - 15, dated 11/03/2026, under
Is being issued as per the approval received.
8. The order is digitally signed and attested.
Available on the Government of Maharashtra website www.maharashtra.gov.in
